text.title: quality-attributes-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for quality-attributes-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Workshop produces 30 scenarios when 5–7 drivers would suffice. Teams over-enumerate second-order concerns and dilute focus.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scenario inflation makes utility trees unwieldy. Prioritization becomes impossible when everything is (H,H). Cap: ≤7 quality attributes, ≤3 scenarios per attribute as drivers.

failure-mode.description: Symmetric utility tree where every scenario is (H,H). Teams generate this because "everything matters" and avoid the hard prioritization conversation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A tree full of (H,H) nodes is not a tree — it is a flat list with no signal. Architecture can be optimized for at most 2–3 conflicting attributes simultaneously; beyond that, you must trade off. Force stakeholders to rank which matters most.

failure-mode.description: Run a one-shot ATAM workshop, generate a 50-page artifact, and file it away. The architecture drifts from the original scenarios as priorities shift; nobody revisits the document.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A static utility tree decays in 6 months. Growth stage, customer feedback, and competitive pressure change importance rankings. Treat the tree as a living document; run quarterly reviews and rotate drivers as needed.

failure-mode.description: Agents invent "industry-standard 99.99% availability for SaaS dashboards" with no citation or grounding in actual data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs hallucinate plausible-sounding SLAs. Force every numeric measure to cite a source: competitor SLA (document it), internal data (link to metrics), vendor SLA, or academic study. If an agent cannot cite, reject the measure.

failure-mode.description: Agents emit 4-part scenarios (skipping Environment or Artifact) or qualitative-only measures ("acceptable response time").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lint with the scenario-lint script. Require all 6 parts and numeric measures before the scenario enters the utility tree.

failure-mode.description: Agents generate symmetric utility trees (everything is H,H) or scenarios for every ISO characteristic, leading to scenario inflation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cap the count: at most 7 quality attributes, at most 3 (H,H) drivers per tree. Force agents to pick the most impactful scenarios and group lower-priority ones under "tracked, not driving."

failure-mode.description: Agents derive Importance from technical difficulty ("this is hard, so it must be important for business").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Importance is stakeholder business judgment; Difficulty is implementation cost. They are independent. Always require human sign-off on Importance before the tree drives architecture.

failure-mode.description: Agents reference obsolete standards like ISO 9126 (superseded in 2001) or ISO/IEC 25010:2011 instead of the current 2023 edition.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the standard version in prompts: "Use ISO/IEC 25010:2023 quality characteristics exclusively."

failure-mode.description: Agents ignore accessibility and safety because training data is biased toward performance and security; the resulting tree is incomplete.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require at least 1 scenario per ISO/IEC 25010 characteristic, including accessibility and safety-critical attributes. If an agent skips them, reject the tree and re-run with explicit coverage requirements.
